pages.full_text:
Saturday.
We set off, cycling along the raised embankment next to the R. Arun. Hardgoing because of June grasses & thistles. We could see Arundel Castle in
[photo of Arundel Cathedral]
Arundel R.C. Cathedral. Gate of mercy.
Nice church.
Philip Howard
this dog.
photo_captions: Arundel R.C. Cathedral. Gate of mercy. Nice church.
pages.notes: Text continues onto right page (mid-sentence 'Arundel Castle in ...'). Photo shows Arundel Cathedral from distance. 'Philip Howard' noted beside the photo — likely a marginal annotation referencing Saint Philip Howard. No handwritten page number visible on this side.

pages.full_text:
the distance which I first mistook for another cement works. It got nearer & nearer. The wind was pushing us.
  We had a coffee & I bought lavender for Venetia whom we've visiting tomorrow in her new house. We also bought pastries for supper. etc
  We caught a train back to Ford Station along the road to the campsite pub for lund which we had booked for 12.30. Good [?Affo] steak & kidney pie.
  After a nap we drove to Chichester & looked at the shops & went to Evensong. Who Should turn up but the Roses! We stopped at Bognor Regis (George V bugger Bognor!) on the way back to the campsite for supper in the van.
  5 0p each tokens for showers. Good Showers.
[photo: The Chagall window (detail)- see 13 Chichester Cathedral]
photo_captions: The Chagall window (detail)- see 13 Chichester Cathedral
pages.notes: Page continues from left page. 'lund' likely means 'lunch'. '[?Affo]' is uncertain — could be 'Affo' as an abbreviation or partial word before 'steak & kidney pie'. 'Good Affo' may mean 'Good Afternoon' or could be a pub name. Photo shows a stained glass window detail labelled 'The Chagall window (detail)- see 13 Chichester Cathedral' with 'Chichester Cathedral' written below. No year can be confirmed from the page text alone — the volume covers 2003–2019, and the reference to 'Venetia' visiting her new house and other contextual clues provide no precise year anchor.
